subject: ELA
title: Literary Devices & Poetry
description: Analysing figurative language, story structure, theme, and poetic devices.

questions.q: 'The thunder grumbled like a grumpy old man.' This is an example of…
What is the THEME of a story?
questions.explanation: Theme = the life lesson or central message the author wants to convey.

questions.q: 'Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.' This is an example of ___.
questions.explanation: Repetition of the same consonant sound ('P') at the start of words = alliteration.

questions.q: In a story's plot, the CLIMAX is...
questions.explanation: The climax is the peak moment of conflict and tension.

questions.q: 'Life is a roller coaster.' This is an example of…
questions.explanation: A direct comparison without 'like' or 'as' = metaphor.

questions.q: What does POINT OF VIEW mean in literature?
options: The perspective from which the story is told
questions.answer: The perspective from which the story is told
questions.explanation: Point of view = who is narrating and from whose perspective we experience the story.

questions.q: A story told using 'I' and 'me' is written in ___ person.
questions.explanation: Using I/me/my throughout = first person narration.

questions.q: Which word is an example of ONOMATOPOEIA?
questions.explanation: Onomatopoeia = words that sound like what they describe. 'Sizzle' sounds like frying.

questions.q: What is the term for the time and place in which a story happens?
questions.explanation: Setting = when and where a story takes place.

questions.q: 'The old house groaned and sighed in the wind.' Which device is used?
questions.explanation: The house is given human actions (groaned, sighed) = personification.
